text:
Once upon a time in a tiny village surrounded by vast forests, there lived a wise old owl named Oliver. Although he couldn't fly like others, Oliver could see farther than anyone else. It was said he could find rainbows.

One fine afternoon, Oliver noticed strange markings on a ancient oak tree near the village pond. He decided to investigate.

Upon further inspection, he found a secret passage. Slowly, he slid down.

Inside, there was stuffy old books! When he opened one, gooey honey spilled out. He found bees! They befriended him.

Together they searched for flowers stronger than normal.

After weeks of searching, they discovered wild honey flowers.

Back she went. Now bees! Owls always knew friendship. But sometimes you gotta think like a bird.

They made beesw honey. Good stuff.

One night, a fox stole some.

Oliver hunted him.

They fought.

He won.

Owl's feather.

Now bees.

Fly away.<|endoftext|><|pad|>
